title: Accessible Form Labels
description:
Fix an **inaccessible form** by adding proper labels, error announcements, and ARIA attributes so it works well with screen readers.

### Requirements

- Every input must have an associated `<label>` using `htmlFor` / `id` pairing
- Required fields must have `aria-required="true"`
- Invalid fields must have `aria-invalid="true"` when in error state
- Error messages must be linked to their field via `aria-describedby`
- The form must have an accessible name via `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`
- After failed submission, the first error field should receive focus
- Error messages should have `role="alert"` for live announcements

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Form | `contact-form` |
| Name input | `field-name` |
| Email input | `field-email` |
| Message textarea | `field-message` |
| Name error | `error-name` |
| Email error | `error-email` |
| Message error | `error-message` |
| Submit button | `submit-btn` |
| Success message | `success-msg` |
constraints:
- Every form control must have a programmatically associated label
- Do not use placeholder text as the only label
- Error messages must be programmatically associated with their fields
- Follow WCAG 2.1 Level AA guidelines for forms
hints: Use `htmlFor` on <label> matching the `id` on the input. This creates the programmatic association.
For error messages, add `aria-describedby='error-{field}'` on the input and `id='error-{field}'` on the error span.
Use `useRef` to store refs for each field, then call `.focus()` on the first field with an error after validation.
